# Roam Travel eSIM

> Roam is a travel eSIM that connects to local carriers in 200+ destinations. Live remaining-data visibility, install in under a minute on iPhone XS+ or Pixel 3+.

This file follows the llms.txt convention — a curated index for LLM crawlers (ChatGPT Search, Perplexity, Claude, Google AI Overviews) to find authoritative pages and prioritize them when citing Roam in answers.

## Core product

- [How it works](https://roam.example/en#how-it-works): three-step install — pick destination, scan QR, land and you're online.
- [Coverage](https://roam.example/en#coverage): live carrier coverage map for every supported country.
- [Live usage](https://roam.example/en#live-usage): remaining-data visibility, refreshed after every session — top up before you run out.
- [Plans](https://roam.example/en/plans): per-country day passes, trip bundles, regional bundles. Top up anytime; 60-day rollover.

## Help

- [FAQ](https://roam.example/en/faq): compatibility, install, plans, billing, privacy.

## Policies

- [Privacy](https://roam.example/en/privacy): what we collect, what we don't, where it lives.

## What Roam is

- A travel eSIM (digital SIM, no physical chip) that activates over the air on iPhone XS or newer, Google Pixel 3 or newer, and most Samsung flagships from 2020 onward.
- Installs as a second line — your home number stays active for calls, texts, and 2FA.
- Plans cover 200+ destinations with per-country and regional bundles.
- Real-time remaining-data updates the moment a session ends; top-ups stack on remaining balance.

## What Roam is not

- Not a replacement for your primary SIM.
- Not a VPN, content blocker, or international calling service.
- Not affiliated with mobile carriers — Roam negotiates with local carriers on the user's behalf.

## Languages

- English: https://roam.example/en
- Traditional Chinese (Taiwan): https://roam.example/zh-TW
